title: Micropolypodium basiattenuatum
taxonomy: Micropolypodium basiattenuatum is classified under the family Polypodiaceae and the genus Micropolypodium. It is a species of fern that belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta and the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Micropolypodium basiattenuatum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. This species is native to certain regions and is part of the vast and diverse kingdom of plants. The classification of Micropolypodium basiattenuatum is essential for understanding its characteristics and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Micropolypodium basiattenuatum, also known as Dulong or Chinese eagle fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions of China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. Bioactive constituents of Micropolypodium basiattenuatum include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of medicinal extracts and supplements.
